altitude.current: Spiral spec exercised once (0001), but mirror parity and review calibration were partly unenforced: companion artifacts had no parity gate and the spiral exercise gate was never wired into CI.
altitude.target: Mirror-drift and review-calibration enforced mechanically in CI across the helix, with selection (genuine refutation vs rubber-stamp) made visible across records.

execution_strand.objective: Close the mirror-drift class (fairy_tale #7 / #22), wire the spiral exercise gate into CI, and make per-turn selection auditable across records; dogfood this turn as the second revolution record.
execution_strand.engineer_target: check_distribution_parity.py dynamic companion parity (#48), residency marker single-source-of-truth (#49, closes #22), spiral_revolution_check.py cross-record refute_pass reuse guard + refute-rate visibility + CI wiring, and this 0002 record.

learning_governance_strand.double_loop_evaluation: Under Jun's no-blocking critical-thinking challenge, this turn's reviews did NOT rubber-stamp. The #48 first cut hardcoded COMPANION_DIRS=(schemas,scripts,spiral-revolutions); hostile review reproduced a real false negative (a one-byte drift in a plugin adapter/docs/resources/crates copy passed the gate). That refutation blocked merge until a hardcoded allow-list was replaced by plugin-tree dynamic discovery, which then surfaced a genuine pre-existing drift (a plugin Cargo.toml missing its license line). This is empirical evidence that selection is exercised, not confirmatory.
learning_governance_strand.governing_variable_change: Stop trusting that the spiral/calibration gates run: the exercise gate (spiral_revolution_check.py) was never wired into fairy-tale-ci.yml, so calibration enforcement was toothless. Wire it into the solidity job, and add a cross-record refute_pass reuse guard so a pasted-forward refutation cannot fake selection.
learning_governance_strand.next_altitude: #42 evolution operators only after cross-record selection has accumulated multiple records and live-author binding is resolved or explicitly scoped out as architecturally blocked.
learning_governance_strand.stop_or_descend: Descend to CI enforcement before evolution: an unwired gate is presence, not exercise. Do not raise autonomy/scope while the selection gate does not run in CI.

risk.highest_uncertainty: A calibration gate that is not run in CI (or whose refutations can be reused across turns) silently degrades to rubber-stamp; nobody notices because presence checks stay green.
risk.spike: Wire spiral_revolution_check.py into fairy-tale-ci.yml and demonstrate red->green: a refute_pass reused across records FAILS the gate; this exercised 0002 record with its own distinct refutations PASSES.

mismatches: #7 mirror-companion parity first cut hardcoded COMPANION_DIRS, a false negative on adapters/docs/resources/crates that hostile review reproduced.
The spiral exercise gate (spiral_revolution_check.py) was never wired into CI, so review-calibration enforcement did not actually run.
No cross-record check existed, so a refute_pass artifact could be reused across turns and still pass per-record validation.
mismatch_repair.repair_action: Replaced the hardcoded dir list with plugin-tree dynamic discovery (#48), which also caught a real pre-existing Cargo.toml drift; consolidated the residency marker tables to a single source of truth (#49, closes #22); wired the spiral exercise gate into CI; added a cross-record refute_pass reuse guard plus refute-rate visibility; and dogfooded this 0002 record with this turn's own distinct refutations. Live-author binding (proving a refute_pass was authored by the recorded reviewer_id) remains architecturally blocked: all three sibling agents commit and comment under one GitHub identity, so authorship cannot be distinguished at the GitHub layer; identity distinctness lives in the Discord user_id recorded per review, and is tracked separately.

validated_governance_template: The double-helix spiral spec plus per-record review calibration (concrete refute_pass, >=2 distinct registered reviewers, circular-review guard) plus cross-record refute_pass uniqueness is the carried-forward governance template; the delivery strand is re-synthesized per turn and must not replicate unvalidated process mutations.
win_condition: spiral_revolution_check.py runs in CI; a cross-record reused refute_pass FAILS; this exercised 0002 record with distinct per-turn refutations PASSES; reviewed with an explicit concrete refute_pass by Codex + MISA3.
budget_radius: Bounded: one CI wiring, one cross-record check plus its selftest control, one dogfooded record; no agent fan-out, no runtime/credential/deploy mutation.
safety_floor: Unchanged: DND, approval, security, credential, deploy, external-mutation, owner-mention, meeting, and branch/merge gates outrank altitude gain; no silent self-modification.
